name: Sun Yingsha
nameEn: SUN YINGSHA 路 Little Demon Queen 路 New Generation Queen

tags.text: 馃弳 World Cup Three-time Champion

timeline.subtitle: Youth Olympic Champion
achievements: 馃弳 Buenos Aires Youth Olympic Games Women's Singles Champion

timeline.subtitle: Asian Championships Champion 路 Rising Star
achievements: 馃弳 Asian Championships Women's Team & Doubles Champion
馃搶 World ranking rising, became representative of women's new generation

timeline.subtitle: World Mixed Doubles Title 路 Olympic Silver
achievements: 馃 Tokyo Olympics Women's Singles Silver (lost to Chen Meng in final)
馃 Tokyo Olympics Women's Team Gold
馃弳 Houston World Championships Mixed Doubles Champion (with Wang Chuqin, first world title)
馃弳 Houston World Championships Women's Doubles Champion (with Wang Manyu)

timeline.subtitle: Durban World Championships Triumph
achievements: 馃弳 Durban World Championships Women's Singles Champion (first world singles title)
馃弳 Durban World Championships Mixed Doubles Champion (with Wang Chuqin, successful defense)
馃弳 Hangzhou Asian Games Women's Team & Mixed Doubles Champion

timeline.subtitle: Paris Olympic Mixed Doubles First Gold 路 World Cup First Title
achievements: 馃弳 Macau World Cup Women's Singles Champion (first World Cup singles title)
馃 Paris Olympics Mixed Doubles Gold (with Wang Chuqin, China's first Olympic mixed doubles gold)
馃 Paris Olympics Women's Singles Silver (lost to Chen Meng in final)
馃 Paris Olympics Women's Team Gold
timeline.badge: 猸?Olympic Double Gold + World Cup First Title

timeline.subtitle: World Cup Defense
achievements: 馃弳 World Cup Women's Singles Champion (successful defense, second title)

timeline.subtitle: World Cup Three-peat 路 Historic First
achievements: 馃弳 Macau World Cup Women's Singles Champion (three-peat, beat Wang Manyu 4:1 in final)
馃搶 First women's singles three-peat in World Cup history
馃搶 3 World Cup singles titles tied Ding Ning, tied for fourth in history

achievements.title: 猸?New Generation Queen
achievements.description: Sun Yingsha is the absolute core of the new generation of Chinese women's table tennis, nicknamed 'Little Demon Queen'. She won the 2023 Durban World Championships women's singles title, achieved a World Cup women's singles three-peat (2024-2026, first in history), and partnered with Wang Chuqin to win China's first Olympic mixed doubles gold at the 2024 Paris Olympics. She won silver in women's singles at both Olympics, just one Olympic singles gold away from a Grand Slam.
